title: Consultant - Single User Plan.
excerpt:

posts.author: Vern (Vern)
posts.cookedHtml:
I know Monday.com currently starts with a 3-user minimum, and I totally get that it’s designed for teams. That said, I wanted to float an idea and see if others might find it useful too. A Consultant User Plan.

I’m a freelance consultant working with a firm that uses Monday.com. They’ve asked me to build and share a project timeline using the platform—specifically a Gantt chart. Since I’m not an employee, they can’t assign me a user licence from their account, and as a solo consultant, it feels a bit steep to purchase a 3-user plan just to collaborate on one timeline.

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there!

That’s a really thoughtful suggestion—and I can see how as a Consultant having a plan could be super useful in cases like yours. Since you’re supporting a team using Monday.com but not officially part of it, it might be worth checking a couple of things:

  • Are you listed as a Monday.com Partner or external collaborator? If so, you might be eligible to access their account without needing a full user license.

  • Could the team temporarily grant you access as a guest or viewer? Depending on their plan, this might be possible for limited collaboration.

  • I’d also recommend reaching out to their Customer Success Manager (CSM) or Monday.com support directly—they might be able to offer a temporary access for consultants like yourself.

Hope that helps!
